info.title: D-ID API
info.description: D-ID API for creating talking avatar videos, AI agents, streaming conversations, and animations.

created_by.description: The user id of the user that created the talk

created_at.description: Talk creation time as iso-8601 string

value.description: user has no permission for stitch
Automatic content moderation - contact support if you would like to submit for manual review

parameters.description: Use your own ElevenLabs API key for TTS (IVC voices only), consuming your ElevenLabs quota. Example: {"elevenlabs": "ABCDEFGH123456"}
parameters.name: x-api-key-external

source_url.description:
The URL of the source image to be animated by the driver video, or a selection from the list of provided
studio actors.

provider.description: text-to-speech provider from list of supported providers. default is microsoft tts

voice_id.description: Voice ID selected from the list of available voices.</br>Retrieve available options from the GET /voices endpoint.

style.description:
The style of the voice.
Available styles change between voices.

rate.description:
The speed of the voice.
The value is relative to 1, 0.5 being half speed, 2 being twice as fast, etc.
Another option is a constant value from x-slow/slow/medium/fast/x-fast.

pitch.description:
The pitch of the voice.
Value could be an absolute value in Hz (including units), a relative value in Hz or st(semitones)
or a constant value from x-low/low/medium/high/x-high.

language.description: Voice customization options. To save the language of the selected agent voice

voice_id.description: Voice ID selected from the list of available voices.</br>Retrieve available options from the GET /voices endpoint.

voice_config.description: Voice customization options. Read more here: https://docs.elevenlabs.io/speech-synthesis/voice-settings

stability.description:
How stable the voice is and the randomness of each new generation.
If ElevenLabs V3 model is selected, must be one of these values: 0.0, 0.5, 1.0 (0.0 = Creative, 0.5 = Natural, 1.0 = Robust).

similarity_boost.description: The similarity slider dictates how closely the AI should adhere to the original voice when attempting to replicate it.

rate.description:
The speed of the voice.
The value is relative to 1. Valid range is 0.7 to 1.2.

style.description:
Determines the style exaggeration of the voice.
This setting attempts to amplify the style of the original speaker.
It does consume additional computational resources and might increase latency if set to anything other than 0.
Valid range is 0 to 1.

use_speaker_boost.description:
boosts the similarity to the original speaker.
Using this setting requires a slightly higher computational load, which in turn increases latency.

apply_language_text_normalization.description:
Controls language text normalization. This helps with proper pronunciation of text in some supported languages.
WARNING: This parameter can heavily increase the latency of the request. Currently only supported for Japanese.

model_id.description: The model id from the list of available models. https://elevenlabs.io/docs/speech-synthesis/models.

language.description: Voice customization options. To save the language of the selected voice

voice_id.description: Voice ID selected from the list of available voices.</br>Retrieve available options from the GET /voices endpoint.

language.description: Voice customization options. To save the language of the selected agent voice

voice_id.description: Voice ID selected from the list of available voices.</br>Retrieve available options from the GET /voices endpoint.

provider.title: Text to Speech Providers

input.description:
The input text that will be synthesized to an audio file.
Note that each provider has its own limitations on the text length.
Maximum 40K characters (10K excluding SSML tags).
input.example: Making videos is easy with D-ID
input.default: Making videos is easy with D-ID

ssml.description: Is the text provided in ssml form.

audio_url.description:
The URL of the audio file which will be used by the actor.
File size is limited to 15MB.
Audio length is limited 5 minutes for clips and 10 minutes for talks.

config.description: Advanced configuration options

url.description: https url to an ARGB jpg/png image, a default logo is used otherwise

position.description: position of the logo in pixels from the top left corner (w,h) negative values are subtracted from last pixel

stitch.description: Stitch back the animated result to the original image

result_format.description: File format of the animated result

fluent.description: Interpolate between the last & first frames of the driver video

pad_audio.description:
Pad the audio with silence at the end (given in seconds)
Will increase the video duration & the credits it consumes

driver_expressions.description:
timed expressions for animation, the current expression is not cleared until a new expression is defined.
Possible expressions include `happy`, `neutral` and more.

start_frame.description: The frame number for the expression start point.

intensity.description: Controls the intensity you want for this expression (between 0 no expression, 1 maximum)

transition_frames.description: Number of frames to use for the transition between expressions

config.title: Configuration

user_data.description: Non-sensitive custom data that will be added to the talk response and webhook

name.description: The name of the talk video

webhook.description:
A webhook URL for sending the payload including animate details
In a case of empty value, the webhook will not be triggered

result_url.description: The URL of the talk video, if not provided use default destination.

driver_url.description: The URL of the driver video to drive the talk, if not provided a driver video will be selected for you from the predefined DriversBank

input.example: Making videos is easy with D-ID

ssml.description: Is the text provided in ssml form.

audio_url.description:
The URL of the audio file which will be used by the actor.
File size is limited to 15MB.
Audio length is limited 5 minutes for clips and 10 minutes for talks.

config.description: Advanced configuration options.

url.description: https url to an ARGB jpg/png image, a default logo is used otherwise

position.description: position of the logo in pixels from the top left corner (w,h) negative values are subtracted from last pixel

result_format.description: File format of the animated result

output_resolution.description:
The output resolution sets the maximum height or width of the output video.
The aspect ratio is preserved from the source image.

config.title: Configuration

rectangle.description:
A set of numbers between 0 and 1 representing the top, left, right and bottom of the crop.
Each number correlates to the distance from the top left corner of the image, relative the the image width.
For example, right: 0.75 means that the right side of the crop is 75% of the image width from the left side.

background.description: Background color of the clip result

anyOf.description: Background color of the animated result, must be a valid 6-characters long color css code.
anyOf.title: Color

anyOf.description: Set 'false' to use transparent background (supported only with result_format = 'webm')
anyOf.title: No Background

color.description: Background color of the animated result, or false to use transparent background in-case of webm result format.

source_url.description: URL to image that will be used as background.

background.title: Background

user_data.description: Non-sensitive custom data that will be added to the clip response and webhook.

languages.description:
The languages into which the video will be translated. Supported languages: Arabic, Bulgarian, Chinese, Croatian, Czech, Danish, Dutch, English, Filipino, Finnish, French, German, Greek,
                     Hindi, Indonesian, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Malay, Polish, Portuguese, Romanian, Russian, Slovak, Spanish,
                     Swedish, Tamil, Turkish and Ukrainian.

source_language.description:
The language spoken in the original video uploaded. Using this feature might help mitigate STT errors. Supported languages: Arabic, Bulgarian, Chinese, Croatian, Czech, Danish, Dutch, English, Filipino, Finnish, French, German, Greek,
                     Hindi, Indonesian, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Malay, Polish, Portuguese, Romanian, Russian, Slovak, Spanish,
                     Swedish, Tamil, Turkish and Ukrainian.

voice_id.description: Voice ID selected from the list of available voices.</br>Retrieve available options from the GET /voices endpoint.

style.description:
The style of the voice.
Available styles change between voices.

rate.description:
The speed of the voice.
The value is relative to 1, 0.5 being half speed, 2 being twice as fast, etc.
Another option is a constant value from x-slow/slow/medium/fast/x-fast.

pitch.description:
The pitch of the voice.
Value could be an absolute value in Hz (including units), a relative value in Hz or st(semitones)
or a constant value from x-low/low/medium/high/x-high.

language.description: Voice customization options. To save the language of the selected agent voice

voice_id.description: Voice ID selected from the list of available voices.</br>Retrieve available options from the GET /voices endpoint.

voice_config.description: Voice customization options. Read more here: https://docs.elevenlabs.io/speech-synthesis/voice-settings

stability.description:
How stable the voice is and the randomness of each new generation.
If ElevenLabs V3 model is selected, must be one of these values: 0.0, 0.5, 1.0 (0.0 = Creative, 0.5 = Natural, 1.0 = Robust).

similarity_boost.description: The similarity slider dictates how closely the AI should adhere to the original voice when attempting to replicate it.

rate.description:
The speed of the voice.
The value is relative to 1. Valid range is 0.7 to 1.2.

style.description:
Determines the style exaggeration of the voice.
This setting attempts to amplify the style of the original speaker.
It does consume additional computational resources and might increase latency if set to anything other than 0.
Valid range is 0 to 1.

use_speaker_boost.description:
boosts the similarity to the original speaker.
Using this setting requires a slightly higher computational load, which in turn increases latency.

apply_language_text_normalization.description:
Controls language text normalization. This helps with proper pronunciation of text in some supported languages.
WARNING: This parameter can heavily increase the latency of the request. Currently only supported for Japanese.

Audio length is limited 5 minutes for clips and 10 minutes for talks.

presenter.description:

Types of presenters below, you could choose 1 option

voice_id.description: Voice ID selected from the list of available voices.</br>Retrieve available options from the GET /voices endpoint.

style.description:
The style of the voice.
Available styles change between voices.

rate.description:
The speed of the voice.
The value is relative to 1, 0.5 being half speed, 2 being twice as fast, etc.
Another option is a constant value from x-slow/slow/medium/fast/x-fast.

pitch.description:
The pitch of the voice.
Value could be an absolute value in Hz (including units), a relative value in Hz or st(semitones)
or a constant value from x-low/low/medium/high/x-high.

language.description: Voice customization options. To save the language of the selected agent voice

voice_id.description: Voice ID selected from the list of available voices.</br>Retrieve available options from the GET /voices endpoint.

voice_config.description: Voice customization options. Read more here: https://docs.elevenlabs.io/speech-synthesis/voice-settings

stability.description:
How stable the voice is and the randomness of each new generation.
If ElevenLabs V3 model is selected, must be one of these values: 0.0, 0.5, 1.0 (0.0 = Creative, 0.5 = Natural, 1.0 = Robust).

similarity_boost.description: The similarity slider dictates how closely the AI should adhere to the original voice when attempting to replicate it.

rate.description:
The speed of the voice.
The value is relative to 1. Valid range is 0.7 to 1.2.

style.description:
Determines the style exaggeration of the voice.
This setting attempts to amplify the style of the original speaker.
It does consume additional computational resources and might increase latency if set to anything other than 0.
Valid range is 0 to 1.

use_speaker_boost.description:
boosts the similarity to the original speaker.
Using this setting requires a slightly higher computational load, which in turn increases latency.

apply_language_text_normalization.description:
Controls language text normalization. This helps with proper pronunciation of text in some supported languages.
WARNING: This parameter can heavily increase the latency of the request. Currently only supported for Japanese.

model_id.description: The model id from the list of available models. https://elevenlabs.io/docs/speech-synthesis/models.

language.description: Voice customization options. To save the language of the selected voice

access.description:
The access level of the requested voice.
Use `external-private` together with the `x-api-key-external` for your own ElevenLabs voices.

llm.description: Configuration for the Large Language Model used by the Agent to generate responses.

instructions.description: Defines what the Agent does and how it should behave.

template.description:
How the Agent uses knowledge when generating responses.
- RAG-Grounded: Uses only provided knowledge
- RAG-Ungrounded: Uses provided knowledge and general model knowledge
- Assistant: Uses general model knowledge only

prompt_customization.description: Advanced configuration for the language model.

role.description: Role the Agent assumes when responding.

personality.description: Personality traits reflected in the Agent’s responses.

topics_to_avoid.description: List of topics the Agent should avoid discussing.

max_response_length.description:
Approximate maximum response length, in words.
Default: 75

knowledge_source.description:
Defines how the Agent receives knowledge.</br>
- base_knowledge — Uses inline knowledge from prompt_knowledge
- documents — Uses knowledge_id (RAG)
(Default: documents)

prompt_knowledge.description: Defines the factual knowledge the Agent uses to answer questions.

prompt_customization.title: Prompt

temperature.description:
Controls how predictable or creative the Agent’s responses are.
Default values by template:
RAG-Grounded: 0.1
RAG-Ungrounded: 0.5
Assistant: 0.5

D-ID and Google providers are only supported with Expressive Avatar presenters.

anyOf.description: Create-request shapes: `provider` is required. Plain interfaces (no Omit/Pick) so TSOA can generate routes.

instructions.description: Defines what the Agent does and how it should behave.

template.description:
How the Agent uses knowledge when generating responses.
- RAG-Grounded: Uses only provided knowledge
- RAG-Ungrounded: Uses provided knowledge and general model knowledge
- Assistant: Uses general model knowledge only

prompt_customization.description: Advanced configuration for the language model.

role.description: Role the Agent assumes when responding.

personality.description: Personality traits reflected in the Agent’s responses.

topics_to_avoid.description: List of topics the Agent should avoid discussing.

max_response_length.description:
Approximate maximum response length, in words.
Default: 75

knowledge_source.description:
Defines how the Agent receives knowledge.</br>
- base_knowledge — Uses inline knowledge from prompt_knowledge
- documents — Uses knowledge_id (RAG)
(Default: documents)

prompt_knowledge.description: Defines the factual knowledge the Agent uses to answer questions.

prompt_customization.title: Prompt

temperature.description:
Controls how predictable or creative the Agent’s responses are.
Default values by template:
RAG-Grounded: 0.1
RAG-Ungrounded: 0.5
Assistant: 0.5

llm.title: LLm Create DTO

knowledge.description:
Knowledge Base used by the Agent to retrieve answers (RAG).
Use the ID returned by the Create Knowledge endpoint.

starter_message.description: List of suggested questions shown to the user at the start of the chat.

greetings.description:
Greeting messages used when the chat starts.
One greeting is selected at random.

embed.description: Enables the Agent for use with the SDK or website embedding via D-ID Studio.

triggers.description: Event-based trigger configuration for the Agent.

chat/end.description: Configuration triggered when a chat session ends.

url.description: Target URL that receives the webhook request.

webhooks.description: Webhooks executed when the event occurs.

allOf.description: Construct a type with the properties of T except for those in type K.

presenter.description:
Presenter who will show in the chat with the user
Types of presenters below, you could choose 1 option

voice_id.description: Voice ID selected from the list of available voices.</br>Retrieve available options from the GET /voices endpoint.

style.description:
The style of the voice.
Available styles change between voices.

rate.description:
The speed of the voice.
The value is relative to 1, 0.5 being half speed, 2 being twice as fast, etc.
Another option is a constant value from x-slow/slow/medium/fast/x-fast.

pitch.description:
The pitch of the voice.
Value could be an absolute value in Hz (including units), a relative value in Hz or st(semitones)
or a constant value from x-low/low/medium/high/x-high.

language.description: Voice customization options. To save the language of the selected agent voice

voice_id.description: Voice ID selected from the list of available voices.</br>Retrieve available options from the GET /voices endpoint.

voice_config.description: Voice customization options. Read more here: https://docs.elevenlabs.io/speech-synthesis/voice-settings

stability.description:
How stable the voice is and the randomness of each new generation.
If ElevenLabs V3 model is selected, must be one of these values: 0.0, 0.5, 1.0 (0.0 = Creative, 0.5 = Natural, 1.0 = Robust).

similarity_boost.description: The similarity slider dictates how closely the AI should adhere to the original voice when attempting to replicate it.

rate.description:
The speed of the voice.
The value is relative to 1. Valid range is 0.7 to 1.2.

style.description:
Determines the style exaggeration of the voice.
This setting attempts to amplify the style of the original speaker.
It does consume additional computational resources and might increase latency if set to anything other than 0.
Valid range is 0 to 1.

use_speaker_boost.description:
boosts the similarity to the original speaker.
Using this setting requires a slightly higher computational load, which in turn increases latency.

apply_language_text_normalization.description:
Controls language text normalization. This helps with proper pronunciation of text in some supported languages.
WARNING: This parameter can heavily increase the latency of the request. Currently only supported for Japanese.

model_id.description: The model id from the list of available models. https://elevenlabs.io/docs/speech-synthesis/models.

language.description: Voice customization options. To save the language of the selected voice

access.description:
The access level of the requested voice.
Use `external-private` together with the `x-api-key-external` for your own ElevenLabs voices.

voice_id.description: Voice ID selected from the list of available voices.</br>Retrieve available options from the GET /voices endpoint.

How stable the voice is and the randomness of each new generation.
If ElevenLabs V3 model is selected, must be one of these values: 0.0, 0.5, 1.0 (0.0 = Creative, 0.5 = Natural, 1.0 = Robust).

similarity_boost.description: The similarity slider dictates how closely the AI should adhere to the original voice when attempting to replicate it.

rate.description:
The speed of the voice.
The value is relative to 1. Valid range is 0.7 to 1.2.

style.description:
Determines the style exaggeration of the voice.
This setting attempts to amplify the style of the original speaker.
It does consume additional computational resources and might increase latency if set to anything other than 0.
Valid range is 0 to 1.

use_speaker_boost.description:
boosts the similarity to the original speaker.
Using this setting requires a slightly higher computational load, which in turn increases latency.

apply_language_text_normalization.description:
Controls language text normalization. This helps with proper pronunciation of text in some supported languages.
WARNING: This parameter can heavily increase the latency of the request. Currently only supported for Japanese.

model_id.description: The model id from the list of available models. https://elevenlabs.io/docs/speech-synthesis/models.

language.description: Voice customization options. To save the language of the selected voice

access.description:
The access level of the requested voice.
Use `external-private` together with the `x-api-key-external` for your own ElevenLabs voices.

voice_id.description: Voice ID selected from the list of available voices.</br>Retrieve available options from the GET /voices endpoint.

style.description:
The style of the voice.
Available styles change between voices.

rate.description:
The speed of the voice.
The value is relative to 1, 0.5 being half speed, 2 being twice as fast, etc.
Another option is a constant value from x-slow/slow/medium/fast/x-fast.

pitch.description:
The pitch of the voice.
Value could be an absolute value in Hz (including units), a relative value in Hz or st(semitones)
or a constant value from x-low/low/medium/high/x-high.

language.description: Voice customization options. To save the language of the selected agent voice

presenter_id.description:
ID of the video avatar used by the Agent.
Retrieved from the GET/ presenters endpoint.

source_url.description: Image URL used as the Avatar background.

llm.description: Configuration for the Large Language Model used by the Agent to generate responses.

instructions.description: Defines what the Agent does and how it should behave.

template.description:
How the Agent uses knowledge when generating responses.
- RAG-Grounded: Uses only provided knowledge
- RAG-Ungrounded: Uses provided knowledge and general model knowledge
- Assistant: Uses general model knowledge only

prompt_customization.description: Advanced configuration for the language model.

role.description: Role the Agent assumes when responding.

personality.description: Personality traits reflected in the Agent’s responses.

topics_to_avoid.description: List of topics the Agent should avoid discussing.

max_response_length.description:
Approximate maximum response length, in words.
Default: 75

knowledge_source.description:
Defines how the Agent receives knowledge.</br>
- base_knowledge — Uses inline knowledge from prompt_knowledge
- documents — Uses knowledge_id (RAG)
(Default: documents)

prompt_knowledge.description: Defines the factual knowledge the Agent uses to answer questions.

prompt_customization.title: Prompt

temperature.description:
Controls how predictable or creative the Agent’s responses are.
Default values by template:
RAG-Grounded: 0.1
RAG-Ungrounded: 0.5
Assistant: 0.5

Available styles change between voices.

rate.description:
The speed of the voice.
The value is relative to 1, 0.5 being half speed, 2 being twice as fast, etc.
Another option is a constant value from x-slow/slow/medium/fast/x-fast.

pitch.description:
The pitch of the voice.
Value could be an absolute value in Hz (including units), a relative value in Hz or st(semitones)
or a constant value from x-low/low/medium/high/x-high.

language.description: Voice customization options. To save the language of the selected agent voice

voice_id.description: Voice ID selected from the list of available voices.</br>Retrieve available options from the GET /voices endpoint.

voice_config.description: Voice customization options. Read more here: https://docs.elevenlabs.io/speech-synthesis/voice-settings

stability.description:
How stable the voice is and the randomness of each new generation.
If ElevenLabs V3 model is selected, must be one of these values: 0.0, 0.5, 1.0 (0.0 = Creative, 0.5 = Natural, 1.0 = Robust).

similarity_boost.description: The similarity slider dictates how closely the AI should adhere to the original voice when attempting to replicate it.

rate.description:
The speed of the voice.
The value is relative to 1. Valid range is 0.7 to 1.2.

style.description:
Determines the style exaggeration of the voice.
This setting attempts to amplify the style of the original speaker.
It does consume additional computational resources and might increase latency if set to anything other than 0.
Valid range is 0 to 1.

use_speaker_boost.description:
boosts the similarity to the original speaker.
Using this setting requires a slightly higher computational load, which in turn increases latency.

apply_language_text_normalization.description:
Controls language text normalization. This helps with proper pronunciation of text in some supported languages.
WARNING: This parameter can heavily increase the latency of the request. Currently only supported for Japanese.

model_id.description: The model id from the list of available models. https://elevenlabs.io/docs/speech-synthesis/models.

language.description: Voice customization options. To save the language of the selected voice

access.description:
The access level of the requested voice.
Use `external-private` together with the `x-api-key-external` for your own ElevenLabs voices.

voice_id.description: Voice ID selected from the list of available voices.</br>Retrieve available options from the GET /voices endpoint.

llm.description:
Configuration for the Large Language Model used by the Agent to generate responses.
D-ID and Google providers are only supported with Expressive Avatar presenters.

instructions.description: Defines what the Agent does and how it should behave.

template.description:
How the Agent uses knowledge when generating responses.
- RAG-Grounded: Uses only provided knowledge
- RAG-Ungrounded: Uses provided knowledge and general model knowledge
- Assistant: Uses general model knowledge only

prompt_customization.description: Advanced configuration for the language model.

role.description: Role the Agent assumes when responding.

personality.description: Personality traits reflected in the Agent’s responses.

topics_to_avoid.description: List of topics the Agent should avoid discussing.

max_response_length.description:
Approximate maximum response length, in words.
Default: 75

knowledge_source.description:
Defines how the Agent receives knowledge.</br>
- base_knowledge — Uses inline knowledge from prompt_knowledge
- documents — Uses knowledge_id (RAG)
(Default: documents)

prompt_knowledge.description: Defines the factual knowledge the Agent uses to answer questions.

prompt_customization.title: Prompt

temperature.description:
Controls how predictable or creative the Agent’s responses are.
Default values by template:
RAG-Grounded: 0.1
RAG-Ungrounded: 0.5
Assistant: 0.5

knowledge.description:
Knowledge Base used by the Agent to retrieve answers (RAG).
Use the ID returned by the Create Knowledge endpoint.

starter_message.description: List of suggested questions shown to the user at the start of the chat.

greetings.description:
Greeting messages used when the chat starts.
One greeting is selected at random.

embed.description: Enables the Agent for use with the SDK or website embedding via D-ID Studio.

triggers.description: Event-based trigger configuration for the Agent.

chat/end.description: Configuration triggered when a chat session ends.

url.description: Target URL that receives the webhook request.

webhooks.description: Webhooks executed when the event occurs.

allOf.description: Construct a type with the properties of T except for those in type K.

post.description: Initiates the creation of a new WebRTC connection to a browser peer. The endpoint returns the necessary information to set up and manage the connection.

fluent.description: Whether to request a fluent stream.

session_timeout.description:
Maximum duration (in seconds) between messages before session times out.
Can only be used with proper permissions

stream_warmup.description:
Whether to stream wamrup video on the connection.
If set to true, will stream a warmup video when connection is established.
At the end of the warmup video, a message containing "stream/ready" will be sent on the data channel.

compatibility_mode.description:
Defines the video codec to be used in the stream.
When set to on: VP8 will be used.
When set to off: H264 will be used
When set to auto the codec will be selected according to the browser.
compatibility_mode.title: Compatibility Mode

post.description: Updates the server with the SDP answer to the SDP offer received from the initial call to the `/agents/{agentId}/streams` endpoint, during the offer/answer negotiation of a WebRTC connection.

session_id.description:
Session identifier information, should be returned in the body of all streaming requests (from the response
of the POST /streams)

answer.description: Jsep answer object used to create a peer connection

type.description:
The SDP contained in the sdp property is the definitive choice in the exchange. In other words, this session
description describes the agreed-upon configuration, and is being sent to finalize negotiation.

sdp.description: Describe the media communication sessions to accept the session the is being negotiated

post.description: Updates the server with a WebRTC ICE candidate. If the candidate parameter is missing or a value of null is given the added ICE candidate is an "end-of-candidates" indicator.

candidate.description:
A string representing the transport address for the candidate that can be used for connectivity checks.
The format of this address is a candidate-attribute as defined in RFC 5245. This string is empty ("") if the
RTCIceCandidate is an "end of candidates" indicator.

sdpMid.description:
A string specifying the candidate's media stream identification tag which uniquely identifies the media stream
within the component with which the candidate is associated, or null if no such association exists.

sdpMLineIndex.description:
If not null, sdpMLineIndex indicates the zero-based index number of the media description (as defined in RFC
4566) in the SDP with which the candidate is associated.

Session identifier information, should be returned in the body of all streaming requests (from the response
of the POST /streams)

provider.description: text-to-speech provider from list of supported providers. default is microsoft tts

voice_id.description: Voice ID selected from the list of available voices.</br>Retrieve available options from the GET /voices endpoint.

style.description:
The style of the voice.
Available styles change between voices.

rate.description:
The speed of the voice.
The value is relative to 1, 0.5 being half speed, 2 being twice as fast, etc.
Another option is a constant value from x-slow/slow/medium/fast/x-fast.

pitch.description:
The pitch of the voice.
Value could be an absolute value in Hz (including units), a relative value in Hz or st(semitones)
or a constant value from x-low/low/medium/high/x-high.

language.description: Voice customization options. To save the language of the selected agent voice

voice_id.description: Voice ID selected from the list of available voices.</br>Retrieve available options from the GET /voices endpoint.

voice_config.description: Voice customization options. Read more here: https://docs.elevenlabs.io/speech-synthesis/voice-settings

stability.description:
How stable the voice is and the randomness of each new generation.
If ElevenLabs V3 model is selected, must be one of these values: 0.0, 0.5, 1.0 (0.0 = Creative, 0.5 = Natural, 1.0 = Robust).

similarity_boost.description: The similarity slider dictates how closely the AI should adhere to the original voice when attempting to replicate it.

rate.description:
The speed of the voice.
The value is relative to 1. Valid range is 0.7 to 1.2.

style.description:
Determines the style exaggeration of the voice.
This setting attempts to amplify the style of the original speaker.
It does consume additional computational resources and might increase latency if set to anything other than 0.
Valid range is 0 to 1.

use_speaker_boost.description:
boosts the similarity to the original speaker.
Using this setting requires a slightly higher computational load, which in turn increases latency.

apply_language_text_normalization.description:
Controls language text normalization. This helps with proper pronunciation of text in some supported languages.
WARNING: This parameter can heavily increase the latency of the request. Currently only supported for Japanese.

model_id.description: The model id from the list of available models. https://elevenlabs.io/docs/speech-synthesis/models.

language.description: Voice customization options. To save the language of the selected voice

access.description:
The access level of the requested voice.
Use `external-private` together with the `x-api-key-external` for your own ElevenLabs voices.

type.description:
The SDP contained in the sdp property is the definitive choice in the exchange. In other words, this session
description describes the agreed-upon configuration, and is being sent to finalize negotiation.

sdp.description: Describe the media communication sessions to accept the session the is being negotiated

post.description: Updates the server with a WebRTC ICE candidate. If the candidate parameter is missing or a value of null is given the added ICE candidate is an "end-of-candidates" indicator.

candidate.description:
A string representing the transport address for the candidate that can be used for connectivity checks.
The format of this address is a candidate-attribute as defined in RFC 5245. This string is empty ("") if the
RTCIceCandidate is an "end of candidates" indicator.

sdpMid.description:
A string specifying the candidate's media stream identification tag which uniquely identifies the media stream
within the component with which the candidate is associated, or null if no such association exists.

sdpMLineIndex.description:
If not null, sdpMLineIndex indicates the zero-based index number of the media description (as defined in RFC
4566) in the SDP with which the candidate is associated.

session_id.description:
Session identifier information, should be returned in the body of all streaming requests (from the response
of the POST /streams)

parameters.description: Use your own ElevenLabs API key for TTS (IVC voices only), consuming your ElevenLabs quota. Example: {"elevenlabs": "ABCDEFGH123456"}
parameters.name: x-api-key-external

driver_url.description:
The URL of the driver video to drive the talk, or a selection from the list or provided drivers.
If not provided a driver video will be selected for you from the predefined drivers bank.

provider.description: text-to-speech provider from list of supported providers. default is microsoft tts

voice_id.description: Voice ID selected from the list of available voices.</br>Retrieve available options from the GET /voices endpoint.

style.description:
The style of the voice.
Available styles change between voices.

rate.description:
The speed of the voice.
The value is relative to 1, 0.5 being half speed, 2 being twice as fast, etc.
Another option is a constant value from x-slow/slow/medium/fast/x-fast.

pitch.description:
The pitch of the voice.
Value could be an absolute value in Hz (including units), a relative value in Hz or st(semitones)
or a constant value from x-low/low/medium/high/x-high.

language.description: Voice customization options. To save the language of the selected agent voice

voice_id.description: Voice ID selected from the list of available voices.</br>Retrieve available options from the GET /voices endpoint.

voice_config.description: Voice customization options. Read more here: https://docs.elevenlabs.io/speech-synthesis/voice-settings

stability.description:
How stable the voice is and the randomness of each new generation.
If ElevenLabs V3 model is selected, must be one of these values: 0.0, 0.5, 1.0 (0.0 = Creative, 0.5 = Natural, 1.0 = Robust).

similarity_boost.description: The similarity slider dictates how closely the AI should adhere to the original voice when attempting to replicate it.

rate.description:
The speed of the voice.
The value is relative to 1. Valid range is 0.7 to 1.2.

style.description:
Determines the style exaggeration of the voice.
This setting attempts to amplify the style of the original speaker.
It does consume additional computational resources and might increase latency if set to anything other than 0.
Valid range is 0 to 1.

use_speaker_boost.description:
boosts the similarity to the original speaker.
Using this setting requires a slightly higher computational load, which in turn increases latency.

apply_language_text_normalization.description:
Controls language text normalization. This helps with proper pronunciation of text in some supported languages.
WARNING: This parameter can heavily increase the latency of the request. Currently only supported for Japanese.

model_id.description: The model id from the list of available models. https://elevenlabs.io/docs/speech-synthesis/models.

language.description: Voice customization options. To save the language of the selected voice

access.description:
The access level of the requested voice.
Use `external-private` together with the `x-api-key-external` for your own ElevenLabs voices.

voice_id.description: Voice ID selected from the list of available voices.</br>Retrieve available options from the GET /voices endpoint.

style.description:
The style of the voice.
Available styles change between voices.

rate.description:
The speed of the voice.
The value is relative to 1, 0.5 being half speed, 2 being twice as fast, etc.
Another option is a constant value from x-slow/slow/medium/fast/x-fast.

pitch.description:
The pitch of the voice.
Value could be an absolute value in Hz (including units), a relative value in Hz or st(semitones)
or a constant value from x-low/low/medium/high/x-high.

language.description: Voice customization options. To save the language of the selected agent voice

input.description:
The input text that will be synthesized to an audio file.
Note that each provider has its own limitations on the text length.
input.example: This is an example text

ssml.description: Is the text provided in ssml form.

audio_url.description:
The URL of the audio file which will be used by the actor.
File size is limit to 15MB.

allOf.description: Construct a type with the properties of T except for those in type K.

stitch.description: Stitch back the animated result to the original image

result_format.description: File format of the animated result

fluent.description: Interpolate between the last & first frames of the driver video

pad_audio.description:
Pad the audio with silence at the end (given in seconds)
Will increase the video duration & the credits it consumes

config.description: Advanced configuration options.

user_data.description: Non-sensitive custom data that will be added to the talk response and webhook.

name.description: The name of the talk video

audio_optimization.description: The optimization level of the audio

metadata.description: Metadata passed to the talks worker

value.description: stream_id doesn't exits or not owened by the user

id.description: Id of the stream - should be returned as *path parameter* in all streaming request to the same stream

jsep.description: Jsep offer object used to create a peer connection

type.description: The session description object describes the initial proposal in an offer/answer exchange.

sdp.description: Describe the media communication sessions to accept the session the is being negotiated

urls.description: URL of the server - can be multiple addresses

credential.description: Credintials for secure connection to the server

ice_servers.description: ICE servers used to create a peer connection

post.description: Initiates the creation of a new WebRTC connection to a browser peer. The endpoint returns the necessary information to set up and manage the connection.

presenter_id.description: ID of the selected presenter.

compatibility_mode.description:
Defines the video codec to be used in the stream.
When set to on: VP8 will be used.
When set to off: H264 will be used
When set to auto the codec will be selected according to the browser.
compatibility_mode.title: Compatibility Mode

stream_warmup.description:
Whether to stream wamrup video on the connection.
If set to true, will stream a warmup video when connection is established.
At the end of the warmup video, a message containing "stream/ready" will be sent on the data channel.

session_timeout.description:
Maximum duration (in seconds) between messages before session times out.
Can only be used with proper permissions

output_resolution.description:
The output resolution sets the maximum height or width of the streamed video.
The aspect ratio is preserved from the source video.
When resolution is not configured, it defaults to the agent output resolution.

fluent.description: Whether to request a fluent stream.

post.description: Updates the server with the SDP answer to the SDP offer received from the initial call to the `/streams` endpoint, during the offer/answer negotiation of a WebRTC connection.

session_id.description:
Session identifier information, should be returned in the body of all streaming requests (from the response
of the POST /streams)

answer.description: Jsep answer object used to create a peer connection

position.description: position of the logo in pixels from the top left corner (w,h) negative values are subtracted from last pixel

result_format.description: File format of the animated result

created_by.description: The user created the clip.

rectangle.description:
A set of numbers between 0 and 1 representing the top, left, right and bottom of the crop.
Each number correlates to the distance from the top left corner of the image, relative the the image width.
For example, right: 0.75 means that the right side of the crop is 75% of the image width from the left side.

color.description: Background color of the animated result, or false to use transparent background in-case of webm result format.

user_data.description: Non-sensitive custom data that will be added to the clip response and webhook.

result_url.description: The URL of the clip video, if not provided use default destination.

The value is relative to 1, 0.5 being half speed, 2 being twice as fast, etc.
Another option is a constant value from x-slow/slow/medium/fast/x-fast.

pitch.description:
The pitch of the voice.
Value could be an absolute value in Hz (including units), a relative value in Hz or st(semitones)
or a constant value from x-low/low/medium/high/x-high.

language.description: Voice customization options. To save the language of the selected agent voice

presenter_id.description: Id of avatar that will be shown for user

source_url.description: Image URL used as the Avatar background.

llm.description: Configuration for the Large Language Model used by the Agent to generate responses.

instructions.description: Defines what the Agent does and how it should behave.

template.description:
How the Agent uses knowledge when generating responses.
- RAG-Grounded: Uses only provided knowledge
- RAG-Ungrounded: Uses provided knowledge and general model knowledge
- Assistant: Uses general model knowledge only

prompt_customization.description: Advanced configuration for the language model.

role.description: Role the Agent assumes when responding.

personality.description: Personality traits reflected in the Agent’s responses.

topics_to_avoid.description: List of topics the Agent should avoid discussing.

max_response_length.description:
Approximate maximum response length, in words.
Default: 75

knowledge_source.description:
Defines how the Agent receives knowledge.</br>
- base_knowledge — Uses inline knowledge from prompt_knowledge
- documents — Uses knowledge_id (RAG)
(Default: documents)

prompt_knowledge.description: Defines the factual knowledge the Agent uses to answer questions.

prompt_customization.title: Prompt

temperature.description:
Controls how predictable or creative the Agent’s responses are.
Default values by template:
RAG-Grounded: 0.1
RAG-Ungrounded: 0.5
Assistant: 0.5

llm.title: Configuration

knowledge.description: Knowledge documents base where the agent will look for answers

provider.description: The provider of the knowledge base

starter_message.description: List of messages to be showen as chat starter questions for the user to ask
List of messages to be showen as chat starter questions for the user to ask

greetings.description:
Greeting messages for the agent
One greeting is randomly displayed at the start of the chat

error.description: Error object in case of errors

chat/end.description: Configuration for a specific trigger event

items.description:
Webhook configuration for immediate execution
Note: HTTP method is controlled by the system, not user-configurable

url.description: Target URL for the webhook

webhooks.description: Webhooks to execute immediately when event fires

type.description:
The SDP contained in the sdp property is the definitive choice in the exchange. In other words, this session
description describes the agreed-upon configuration, and is being sent to finalize negotiation.

sdp.description: Describe the media communication sessions to accept the session the is being negotiated

candidate.description:
A string representing the transport address for the candidate that can be used for connectivity checks.
The format of this address is a candidate-attribute as defined in RFC 5245. This string is empty ("") if the
RTCIceCandidate is an "end of candidates" indicator.

sdpMid.description:
A string specifying the candidate's media stream identification tag which uniquely identifies the media stream
within the component with which the candidate is associated, or null if no such association exists.

sdpMLineIndex.description:
If not null, sdpMLineIndex indicates the zero-based index number of the media description (as defined in RFC
4566) in the SDP with which the candidate is associated.

provider.description: text-to-speech provider from list of supported providers. default is microsoft tts

voice_id.description: Voice ID selected from the list of available voices.</br>Retrieve available options from the GET /voices endpoint.

style.description:
The style of the voice.
Available styles change between voices.

rate.description:
The speed of the voice.
The value is relative to 1, 0.5 being half speed, 2 being twice as fast, etc.
Another option is a constant value from x-slow/slow/medium/fast/x-fast.

pitch.description:
The pitch of the voice.
Value could be an absolute value in Hz (including units), a relative value in Hz or st(semitones)
or a constant value from x-low/low/medium/high/x-high.

language.description: Voice customization options. To save the language of the selected agent voice

voice_id.description: Voice ID selected from the list of available voices.</br>Retrieve available options from the GET /voices endpoint.

voice_config.description: Voice customization options. Read more here: https://docs.elevenlabs.io/speech-synthesis/voice-settings

stability.description:
How stable the voice is and the randomness of each new generation.
If ElevenLabs V3 model is selected, must be one of these values: 0.0, 0.5, 1.0 (0.0 = Creative, 0.5 = Natural, 1.0 = Robust).

similarity_boost.description: The similarity slider dictates how closely the AI should adhere to the original voice when attempting to replicate it.

rate.description:
The speed of the voice.
The value is relative to 1. Valid range is 0.7 to 1.2.

style.description:
Determines the style exaggeration of the voice.
This setting attempts to amplify the style of the original speaker.
It does consume additional computational resources and might increase latency if set to anything other than 0.
Valid range is 0 to 1.

use_speaker_boost.description:
boosts the similarity to the original speaker.
Using this setting requires a slightly higher computational load, which in turn increases latency.

apply_language_text_normalization.description:
Controls language text normalization. This helps with proper pronunciation of text in some supported languages.
WARNING: This parameter can heavily increase the latency of the request. Currently only supported for Japanese.

model_id.description: The model id from the list of available models. https://elevenlabs.io/docs/speech-synthesis/models.

language.description: Voice customization options. To save the language of the selected voice

access.description:
The access level of the requested voice.
Use `external-private` together with the `x-api-key-external` for your own ElevenLabs voices.

rectangle.description:
A set of numbers between 0 and 1 representing the top, left, right and bottom of the crop.
Each number correlates to the distance from the top left corner of the image, relative the the image width.
For example, right: 0.75 means that the right side of the crop is 75% of the image width from the left side.

stability.description:
How stable the voice is and the randomness of each new generation.
If ElevenLabs V3 model is selected, must be one of these values: 0.0, 0.5, 1.0 (0.0 = Creative, 0.5 = Natural, 1.0 = Robust).

similarity_boost.description: The similarity slider dictates how closely the AI should adhere to the original voice when attempting to replicate it.

rate.description:
The speed of the voice.
The value is relative to 1. Valid range is 0.7 to 1.2.

style.description:
Determines the style exaggeration of the voice.
This setting attempts to amplify the style of the original speaker.
It does consume additional computational resources and might increase latency if set to anything other than 0.
Valid range is 0 to 1.

use_speaker_boost.description:
boosts the similarity to the original speaker.
Using this setting requires a slightly higher computational load, which in turn increases latency.

apply_language_text_normalization.description:
Controls language text normalization. This helps with proper pronunciation of text in some supported languages.
WARNING: This parameter can heavily increase the latency of the request. Currently only supported for Japanese.

The speed of the voice.
The value is relative to 1. Valid range is 0.7 to 1.2.

style.description:
Determines the style exaggeration of the voice.
This setting attempts to amplify the style of the original speaker.
It does consume additional computational resources and might increase latency if set to anything other than 0.
Valid range is 0 to 1.

use_speaker_boost.description:
boosts the similarity to the original speaker.
Using this setting requires a slightly higher computational load, which in turn increases latency.

apply_language_text_normalization.description:
Controls language text normalization. This helps with proper pronunciation of text in some supported languages.
WARNING: This parameter can heavily increase the latency of the request. Currently only supported for Japanese.

model_id.description: The model id from the list of available models. https://elevenlabs.io/docs/speech-synthesis/models.

language.description: Voice customization options. To save the language of the selected voice

style.description:
The style of the voice.
Available styles change between voices.

rate.description:
The speed of the voice.
The value is relative to 1, 0.5 being half speed, 2 being twice as fast, etc.
Another option is a constant value from x-slow/slow/medium/fast/x-fast.

pitch.description:
The pitch of the voice.
Value could be an absolute value in Hz (including units), a relative value in Hz or st(semitones)
or a constant value from x-low/low/medium/high/x-high.

language.description: Voice customization options. To save the language of the selected agent voice

provider.title: Text to Speech Providers

input.description:
The input text that will be synthesized to an audio file.
Note that each provider has its own limitations on the text length.
Maximum 40K characters (10K excluding SSML tags).
input.example: Making videos is easy with D-ID
input.default: Making videos is easy with D-ID

ssml.description: Is the text provided in ssml form.

audio_url.description:
The URL of the audio file which will be used by the actor.
File size is limited to 15MB.
Audio length is limited 5 minutes for clips and 10 minutes for talks.

CreateExpressiveRequestDto.description:
Flattens intersection types into a single object type.
This is useful for improving validation error messages and type display.

owner_id.description: The id of the owner of the expressive video

thumbnail_url.description: The thumbnail url of the video

created_at.description: The date the video was created

sentiment_id.description: The chosen sentiment id of the video

llm.description: Configuration for the Large Language Model used by the Agent to generate responses.

instructions.description: Defines what the Agent does and how it should behave.

template.description:
How the Agent uses knowledge when generating responses.
- RAG-Grounded: Uses only provided knowledge
- RAG-Ungrounded: Uses provided knowledge and general model knowledge
- Assistant: Uses general model knowledge only

prompt_customization.description: Advanced configuration for the language model.

role.description: Role the Agent assumes when responding.

personality.description: Personality traits reflected in the Agent’s responses.

topics_to_avoid.description: List of topics the Agent should avoid discussing.

max_response_length.description:
Approximate maximum response length, in words.
Default: 75

knowledge_source.description:
Defines how the Agent receives knowledge.</br>
- base_knowledge — Uses inline knowledge from prompt_knowledge
- documents — Uses knowledge_id (RAG)
(Default: documents)

prompt_knowledge.description: Defines the factual knowledge the Agent uses to answer questions.

prompt_customization.title: Prompt

temperature.description:
Controls how predictable or creative the Agent’s responses are.
Default values by template:
RAG-Grounded: 0.1
RAG-Ungrounded: 0.5
Assistant: 0.5

presenter.description: Defines the Agent’s avatar, including its visual appearance and voice.

voice_id.description: Voice ID selected from the list of available voices.</br>Retrieve available options from the GET /voices endpoint.

style.description:
The style of the voice.
Available styles change between voices.

rate.description:
The speed of the voice.
The value is relative to 1, 0.5 being half speed, 2 being twice as fast, etc.
Another option is a constant value from x-slow/slow/medium/fast/x-fast.

pitch.description:
The pitch of the voice.
Value could be an absolute value in Hz (including units), a relative value in Hz or st(semitones)
or a constant value from x-low/low/medium/high/x-high.

language.description: Voice customization options. To save the language of the selected agent voice

voice_id.description: Voice ID selected from the list of available voices.</br>Retrieve available options from the GET /voices endpoint.

voice_config.description: Voice customization options. Read more here: https://docs.elevenlabs.io/speech-synthesis/voice-settings

stability.description:
How stable the voice is and the randomness of each new generation.
If ElevenLabs V3 model is selected, must be one of these values: 0.0, 0.5, 1.0 (0.0 = Creative, 0.5 = Natural, 1.0 = Robust).

similarity_boost.description: The similarity slider dictates how closely the AI should adhere to the original voice when attempting to replicate it.

rate.description:
The speed of the voice.
The value is relative to 1. Valid range is 0.7 to 1.2.

style.description:
Determines the style exaggeration of the voice.
This setting attempts to amplify the style of the original speaker.
It does consume additional computational resources and might increase latency if set to anything other than 0.
Valid range is 0 to 1.

use_speaker_boost.description:
boosts the similarity to the original speaker.
Using this setting requires a slightly higher computational load, which in turn increases latency.

apply_language_text_normalization.description:
Controls language text normalization. This helps with proper pronunciation of text in some supported languages.
WARNING: This parameter can heavily increase the latency of the request. Currently only supported for Japanese.

model_id.description: The model id from the list of available models. https://elevenlabs.io/docs/speech-synthesis/models.

language.description: Voice customization options. To save the language of the selected voice

access.description:
The access level of the requested voice.
Use `external-private` together with the `x-api-key-external` for your own ElevenLabs voices.

temperature.description:
Controls how predictable or creative the Agent’s responses are.
Default values by template:
RAG-Grounded: 0.1
RAG-Ungrounded: 0.5
Assistant: 0.5

llm.title: LLm Create DTO

knowledge.description:
Knowledge Base used by the Agent to retrieve answers (RAG).
Use the ID returned by the Create Knowledge endpoint.

starter_message.description: List of suggested questions shown to the user at the start of the chat.

greetings.description:
Greeting messages used when the chat starts.
One greeting is selected at random.

embed.description: Enables the Agent for use with the SDK or website embedding via D-ID Studio.

triggers.description: Event-based trigger configuration for the Agent.

chat/end.description: Configuration triggered when a chat session ends.

url.description: Target URL that receives the webhook request.

webhooks.description: Webhooks executed when the event occurs.

source_url.description: Image URL used to create the avatar.

thumbnail.description: Preview image URL for the avatar.

stitch.description:
Controls the output framing.
`true` - Original image framing `false` - Close-up framing

voice_id.description: Voice ID selected from the list of available voices.</br>Retrieve available options from the GET /voices endpoint.

style.description:
The style of the voice.
Available styles change between voices.

rate.description:
The speed of the voice.
The value is relative to 1, 0.5 being half speed, 2 being twice as fast, etc.
Another option is a constant value from x-slow/slow/medium/fast/x-fast.

pitch.description:
The pitch of the voice.
Value could be an absolute value in Hz (including units), a relative value in Hz or st(semitones)
or a constant value from x-low/low/medium/high/x-high.

language.description: Voice customization options. To save the language of the selected agent voice

voice_id.description: Voice ID selected from the list of available voices.</br>Retrieve available options from the GET /voices endpoint.

voice_config.description: Voice customization options. Read more here: https://docs.elevenlabs.io/speech-synthesis/voice-settings

stability.description:
How stable the voice is and the randomness of each new generation.
If ElevenLabs V3 model is selected, must be one of these values: 0.0, 0.5, 1.0 (0.0 = Creative, 0.5 = Natural, 1.0 = Robust).

similarity_boost.description: The similarity slider dictates how closely the AI should adhere to the original voice when attempting to replicate it.

rate.description:
The speed of the voice.
The value is relative to 1. Valid range is 0.7 to 1.2.

style.description:
Determines the style exaggeration of the voice.
This setting attempts to amplify the style of the original speaker.
It does consume additional computational resources and might increase latency if set to anything other than 0.
Valid range is 0 to 1.

use_speaker_boost.description:
boosts the similarity to the original speaker.
Using this setting requires a slightly higher computational load, which in turn increases latency.

apply_language_text_normalization.description:
Controls language text normalization. This helps with proper pronunciation of text in some supported languages.
WARNING: This parameter can heavily increase the latency of the request. Currently only supported for Japanese.

model_id.description: The model id from the list of available models. https://elevenlabs.io/docs/speech-synthesis/models.

language.description: Voice customization options. To save the language of the selected voice

access.description:
The access level of the requested voice.
Use `external-private` together with the `x-api-key-external` for your own ElevenLabs voices.

voice_id.description: Voice ID selected from the list of available voices.</br>Retrieve available options from the GET /voices endpoint.

The value is relative to 1. Valid range is 0.7 to 1.2.

style.description:
Determines the style exaggeration of the voice.
This setting attempts to amplify the style of the original speaker.
It does consume additional computational resources and might increase latency if set to anything other than 0.
Valid range is 0 to 1.

use_speaker_boost.description:
boosts the similarity to the original speaker.
Using this setting requires a slightly higher computational load, which in turn increases latency.

apply_language_text_normalization.description:
Controls language text normalization. This helps with proper pronunciation of text in some supported languages.
WARNING: This parameter can heavily increase the latency of the request. Currently only supported for Japanese.

model_id.description: The model id from the list of available models. https://elevenlabs.io/docs/speech-synthesis/models.

language.description: Voice customization options. To save the language of the selected voice

access.description:
The access level of the requested voice.
Use `external-private` together with the `x-api-key-external` for your own ElevenLabs voices.

voice_id.description: Voice ID selected from the list of available voices.</br>Retrieve available options from the GET /voices endpoint.

style.description:
The style of the voice.
Available styles change between voices.

rate.description:
The speed of the voice.
The value is relative to 1, 0.5 being half speed, 2 being twice as fast, etc.
Another option is a constant value from x-slow/slow/medium/fast/x-fast.

pitch.description:
The pitch of the voice.
Value could be an absolute value in Hz (including units), a relative value in Hz or st(semitones)
or a constant value from x-low/low/medium/high/x-high.

language.description: Voice customization options. To save the language of the selected agent voice

anyOf.description: Make all properties in T optional
anyOf.title: Video Avatar

llm.description:
Configuration for the Large Language Model used by the Agent to generate responses.
D-ID and Google providers are only supported with Expressive Avatar presenters.

instructions.description: Defines what the Agent does and how it should behave.

template.description:
How the Agent uses knowledge when generating responses.
- RAG-Grounded: Uses only provided knowledge
- RAG-Ungrounded: Uses provided knowledge and general model knowledge
- Assistant: Uses general model knowledge only

prompt_customization.description: Advanced configuration for the language model.

role.description: Role the Agent assumes when responding.

personality.description: Personality traits reflected in the Agent’s responses.

topics_to_avoid.description: List of topics the Agent should avoid discussing.

max_response_length.description:
Approximate maximum response length, in words.
Default: 75

knowledge_source.description:
Defines how the Agent receives knowledge.</br>
- base_knowledge — Uses inline knowledge from prompt_knowledge
- documents — Uses knowledge_id (RAG)
(Default: documents)

prompt_knowledge.description: Defines the factual knowledge the Agent uses to answer questions.

prompt_customization.title: Prompt

temperature.description:
Controls how predictable or creative the Agent’s responses are.
Default values by template:
RAG-Grounded: 0.1
RAG-Ungrounded: 0.5
Assistant: 0.5

knowledge.description:
Knowledge Base used by the Agent to retrieve answers (RAG).
Use the ID returned by the Create Knowledge endpoint.

starter_message.description: List of suggested questions shown to the user at the start of the chat.

greetings.description:
Greeting messages used when the chat starts.
One greeting is selected at random.

embed.description: Enables the Agent for use with the SDK or website embedding via D-ID Studio.

triggers.description: Event-based trigger configuration for the Agent.

chat/end.description: Configuration triggered when a chat session ends.

url.description: Target URL that receives the webhook request.

webhooks.description: Webhooks executed when the event occurs.

knowledge_id.description: The ID of the agent's knowledge

message_id.description: The ID of the message to rate

chat_id.description: The ID of the chat containing the message

score.description: The rating score: positive for favorable, negative for unfavorable
The rating score: positive for favorable, negative for unfavorable

message_id.description: The ID of the message to rate

id.description: Id of the stream - should be returned as *path parameter* in all streaming request to the same stream

jsep.description: Jsep offer object used to create a peer connection

type.description: The session description object describes the initial proposal in an offer/answer exchange.

sdp.description: Describe the media communication sessions to accept the session the is being negotiated

urls.description: URL of the server - can be multiple addresses

credential.description: Credintials for secure connection to the server

ice_servers.description: ICE servers used to create a peer connection

fluent.description: Whether to request a fluent stream.

session_timeout.description:
Maximum duration (in seconds) between messages before session times out.
Can only be used with proper permissions

stream_warmup.description:
Whether to stream wamrup video on the connection.
If set to true, will stream a warmup video when connection is established.
At the end of the warmup video, a message containing "stream/ready" will be sent on the data channel.

compatibility_mode.description:
Defines the video codec to be used in the stream.
When set to on: VP8 will be used.
When set to off: H264 will be used
When set to auto the codec will be selected according to the browser.
compatibility_mode.title: Compatibility Mode

session_id.description:
Session identifier information, should be returned in the body of all streaming requests (from the response
of the POST /streams)

answer.description: Jsep answer object used to create a peer connection

type.description:
The SDP contained in the sdp property is the definitive choice in the exchange. In other words, this session
description describes the agreed-upon configuration, and is being sent to finalize negotiation.

sdp.description: Describe the media communication sessions to accept the session the is being negotiated

candidate.description:
A string representing the transport address for the candidate that can be used for connectivity checks.
The format of this address is a candidate-attribute as defined in RFC 5245. This string is empty ("") if the
RTCIceCandidate is an "end of candidates" indicator.

sdpMid.description:
A string specifying the candidate's media stream identification tag which uniquely identifies the media stream
within the component with which the candidate is associated, or null if no such association exists.

sdpMLineIndex.description:
If not null, sdpMLineIndex indicates the zero-based index number of the media description (as defined in RFC
4566) in the SDP with which the candidate is associated.

session_id.description:
Session identifier information, should be returned in the body of all streaming requests (from the response
of the POST /streams)

voice_id.description: Voice ID selected from the list of available voices.</br>Retrieve available options from the GET /voices endpoint.

style.description:

Available styles change between voices.

rate.description:
The speed of the voice.
The value is relative to 1, 0.5 being half speed, 2 being twice as fast, etc.

pitch.description:

How stable the voice is and the randomness of each new generation.

stitch.description: Stitch back the animated result to the original image

compatibility_mode.description:
Defines the video codec to be used in the stream.
When set to on: VP8 will be used.
When set to off: H264 will be used
When set to auto the codec will be selected according to the browser.
compatibility_mode.title: Compatibility Mode

stream_warmup.description:
Whether to stream wamrup video on the connection.
If set to true, will stream a warmup video when connection is established.
At the end of the warmup video, a message containing "stream/ready" will be sent on the data channel.

session_timeout.description:
Maximum duration (in seconds) between messages before session times out.
Can only be used with proper permissions

output_resolution.description:
The output resolution sets the maximum height or width of the streamed video.
The aspect ratio is preserved from the source image.
When resolution is not configured, it defaults to the agent output resolution.

driver_url.description:
The URL of the driver video to drive the talk, or a selection from the list or provided drivers.
If not provided a driver video will be selected for you from the predefined drivers bank.

provider.description: text-to-speech provider from list of supported providers. default is microsoft tts

voice_id.description: Voice ID selected from the list of available voices.</br>Retrieve available options from the GET /voices endpoint.

style.description:
The style of the voice.
Available styles change between voices.

rate.description:
The speed of the voice.
The value is relative to 1, 0.5 being half speed, 2 being twice as fast, etc.
Another option is a constant value from x-slow/slow/medium/fast/x-fast.

pitch.description:
The pitch of the voice.
Value could be an absolute value in Hz (including units), a relative value in Hz or st(semitones)
or a constant value from x-low/low/medium/high/x-high.

language.description: Voice customization options. To save the language of the selected agent voice

voice_id.description: Voice ID selected from the list of available voices.</br>Retrieve available options from the GET /voices endpoint.

voice_config.description: Voice customization options. Read more here: https://docs.elevenlabs.io/speech-synthesis/voice-settings

stability.description:
How stable the voice is and the randomness of each new generation.
If ElevenLabs V3 model is selected, must be one of these values: 0.0, 0.5, 1.0 (0.0 = Creative, 0.5 = Natural, 1.0 = Robust).

similarity_boost.description: The similarity slider dictates how closely the AI should adhere to the original voice when attempting to replicate it.

rate.description:
The speed of the voice.
The value is relative to 1. Valid range is 0.7 to 1.2.

style.description:
Determines the style exaggeration of the voice.
This setting attempts to amplify the style of the original speaker.
It does consume additional computational resources and might increase latency if set to anything other than 0.
Valid range is 0 to 1.

use_speaker_boost.description:
boosts the similarity to the original speaker.
Using this setting requires a slightly higher computational load, which in turn increases latency.

apply_language_text_normalization.description:
Controls language text normalization. This helps with proper pronunciation of text in some supported languages.
WARNING: This parameter can heavily increase the latency of the request. Currently only supported for Japanese.

model_id.description: The model id from the list of available models. https://elevenlabs.io/docs/speech-synthesis/models.

language.description: Voice customization options. To save the language of the selected voice

access.description:
The access level of the requested voice.
Use `external-private` together with the `x-api-key-external` for your own ElevenLabs voices.

rectangle.description:
A set of numbers between 0 and 1 representing the top, left, right and bottom of the crop.
Each number correlates to the distance from the top left corner of the image, relative the the image width.
For example, right: 0.75 means that the right side of the crop is 75% of the image width from the left side.

color.description: Background color of the animated result, or false to use transparent background in-case of webm result format.

user_data.description: Non-sensitive custom data that will be added to the clip response and webhook.

result_url.description: The URL of the clip video, if not provided use default destination.
